zh.lifeSummary: 當Blanche de la Pole於大約1327年出生在英國越可爾赫爾時，她的父親Sir William de la Pole26歲，母親Katherine Norwich25歲。她於1344年與Richard le Scrope 1st Baron Scrope of Bolton在英國越可爾文斯萊達爾結婚。他們至少是6個兒子的父母。她去世於1378年2月18日之前在英國越可爾博爾頓 武朋 斯瓦爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Blanche de la Pole于大约1327年出生在英国越可爾赫爾时，她的父亲Sir William de la Pole26岁，母亲Katherine Norwich25岁。她于1344年与Richard le Scrope 1st Baron Scrope of Bolton在英国越可爾文斯萊達爾结婚。他们至少是6个儿子的父母。她去世于1378年2月18日之前在英国越可爾博爾頓 武朋 斯瓦爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Pole
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Leicestershire): variant of   Paul   or   Poole  .
Americanized form of German   Pohle   or   Pohl  .

variants.name: Poles
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Livio, Primo, Santo.
English and Welsh: variant of Pole and Powell + genitival or excrescent -s.
Italian (Friuli and Veneto): probably a derivative of the personal name   Polo  . It is also found in Slovenia.

variants.name: Paul
meanings: English, French, German, Dutch, Swedish, West Indian (mainly Haiti, also e.g. Saint Lucia), and African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania): from the personal name Paul (from Latin Paulus ‘small’), which has always been popular in Christendom. It was the name adopted by the Pharisee Saul of Tarsus after his conversion to Christianity on the road to Damascus in about AD 34. He was a most energetic missionary to the Gentiles in the Roman Empire, and played a very significant role in establishing Christianity as a major world religion. The name was borne also by numerous other early Christian saints. It is also occasionally borne by Jews; the reasons for this are not clear. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages and their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Greek Pavlis , Slovenian Pavel and Pavlič (see Pavlic ), Polish Paweł (see Pawel ) and Pawlicki , Assyrian/Chaldean Polous and Polus . In France, this surname is most common in Brittany (see 2 below).
Breton (mainly Finistère): from a Frenchified form of the personal name Paol, Breton form of Paul .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Phóil ‘son of Paul’. Compare McFall .

nameMeanings.name: Blanche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a nickname for a blonde, from blanche, feminine of Old French blanc ‘white’ (of Germanic origin). It came to be associated with the notion of whiteness as indicating purity, and was introduced into England as a given name by the Normans. A pale complexion combined with light hair was long an ideal of beauty in Europe (compare Modern English fair, which at first meant ‘beautiful’ and then, from the 16th century, ‘light in colouring’).

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Blanche de la Pole was born about 1327, in Hull, Yorkshire, England, her father, Sir William de la Pole, was 26 and her mother, Katherine Norwich, was 25. She married Richard le Scrope 1st Baron Scrope of Bolton in 1344, in Wensleydale, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons. She died before 18 February 1378, in Bolton upon Swale, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Blanche de la Pole est née environ 1327, à Hull, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Sir William de la Pole, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Katherine Norwich, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Richard le Scrope 1st Baron Scrope of Bolton en 1344, à Wensleydale, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils. Elle est décédée avant 18 février 1378, à Bolton upon Swale, Everwyck, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Blanche de la Pole um 1327 in Hull, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir William de la Pole 26 und ihre Mutter Katherine Norwich 25. Sie hat 1344 in Wensleydale, Yorkshire, England Richard le Scrope 1st Baron Scrope of Bolton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne. Sie starb vor 18. Februar 1378 in Bolton upon Swale, Yorkshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Blanche de la Pole è nata il circa 1327 nel Hull, Yorkshire, England, sua padre, Sir William de la Pole, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Katherine Norwich, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato Richard le Scrope 1st Baron Scrope of Bolton nel 1344, nel Wensleydale, Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli. Morì prima del 18 febbraio 1378, nel Bolton upon Swale, Yorkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Blanche de la Poleが約1327年にイギリスヨークシュアイアハルで生まれた時，父Sir William de la Poleは26歳，母Katherine Norwichは25歳であった。1344年にイギリスヨークシュアイアウエンスリーダルでRichard le Scrope 1st Baron Scrope of Boltonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子の親であった。彼女は1378年2月18日以前にイギリスヨークシュアイアボルトン ウプオン スワルで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Blanche de la Pole(이)가 대략 1327년 영국 요르크실르 훌에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William de la Pole(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Katherine Norwich(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1344년 영국 요르크실르 웬슬리데일에서 Richard le Scrope 1st Baron Scrope of Bolton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1378년 2월 18일 이전 영국 요르크실르 볼턴 업 스월에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Blanche de la Pole nasceu aproximadamente 1327, em Hull, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William de la Pole, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Katherine Norwich, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com Richard le Scrope 1st Baron Scrope of Bolton em 1344, em Wensleydale, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos. Ela faleceu antes 18 de fevereiro de 1378, em Bolton upon Swale, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Blanche de la Pole родилась около 1327 г. в местности хулл, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir William de la Pole было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Katherine Norwich, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard le Scrope 1st Baron Scrope of Bolton в 1344 г. в местности венслидеил, еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей. Она умерла перед 18 февраль 1378 г. в местности болтон упон свеил, еркшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Blanche de la Pole nació aproximadamente 1327, en Hull, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir William de la Pole, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Katherine Norwich, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Richard le Scrope 1st Baron Scrope of Bolton en 1344, en Wensleydale, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos. Murió antes de 18 de febrero de 1378, en Bolton upon Swale, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.
